records.ety: [L. confidere; con- + fidere to trust. See Faith, and cf. Affiance.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Confided; p. pr. & vb. n. Confiding.]

defs: To put faith (in); to repose confidence; to trust; -- usually followed by in; as, the prince confides in his ministers.
quotes: By thy command I rise or fall, In thy protection I confide.
Judge before friendships, then confide till death.

defs: To intrust; to give in charge; to commit to one's keeping; -- followed by to.
quotes: Congress may . . . confide to the Circuit jurisdiction of all offenses against the United States.
